message:
Terminal emulator: wide chars, line flags, and copy extraction

Major terminal emulator improvement adding three interconnected features:

state.rs:
- WIDE_CONTINUATION sentinel (U+E000) for right-hand cells of
  double-width characters, with Cell::is_wide_continuation()
- LineFlag enum (HardNewline/SoftWrap) tracked per grid row via
  line_flags Vec, propagated through resize/clear/scroll operations
- Grid::scroll_up() returns (Vec<Cell>, LineFlag) tuples, preserving
  flags into scrollback
- Scrollback stores (Vec<Cell>, LineFlag) tuples with push_with_flag()
  and line_with_flag() accessors
- put_char_wide() places char + continuation marker with wrapping
- newline() marks rows as HardNewline, put_char() marks SoftWrap on
  auto-wrap
- extract_text() on both TerminalState and Grid: soft-wrapped rows
  joined, hard-newline rows separated by \n, trailing whitespace
  trimmed, wide continuation cells skipped, selection normalization
  and bounds clamping

25+ tests: line flags, wide chars, soft/hard wrap tracking, copy
extraction (single/multi-row, mixed wraps, reversed selection, wide
chars, partial selection, empty grid, out-of-bounds, combining marks,
scrollback flag preservation).

mod.rs: Re-export LineFlag, WIDE_CONTINUATION.
syntax.rs: Clippy fix in TOML tokenizer (manual indexing to iter).

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
